message:
feat(memory_pressure): Cx-first run_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds MemoryPressureMonitor::run_with_cx behind the asupersync-runtime
feature, completing the sampling-loop Cx-first triad (CpuPressureMonitor,
MemoryBudgetManager, MemoryPressureMonitor). All three share the same
lifecycle shape — first-tick skip → sleep(interval) → shutdown check →
sample → log-if-pressure — so their Cx-first variants are structurally
identical.

Scope:

1. crates/frankenterm-core/src/memory_pressure.rs (+122, additions only)

   Adds `MemoryPressureMonitor::run_with_cx(cx: &Cx, shutdown)`.

     * Pre-flight cancellation gate: `cx.is_cancel_requested()` on entry
       returns without sampling.

     * Each inter-sample sleep bound via
       `crate::runtime_compat::sleep_with_cx`, so budget-driven
       cancellation cuts the sleep deterministically under LabRuntime
       virtual time. `Err` from sleep_with_cx terminates the loop
       cleanly without a spurious extra sample.

     * Both `shutdown` flag and `cx.is_cancel_requested()` checked each
       iteration so either cancellation path terminates the loop at
       the next iteration gate.

   Legacy `run` preserved; strictly additive.

   Two new LabRuntime tests under `labruntime_memory_pressure`:

     * `run_with_cx_pre_cancelled_exits_without_sampling` (seed
       0x3E30_BED5_BED5_0303): pre-cancels the Cx; asserts the atomic
       tier handle stays at Green (initial value) and shutdown flag
       untouched. A Cx-unaware loop would sleep on the first interval
       and step-explode LabRuntime's max_steps budget.

     * `run_with_cx_shutdown_set_exits_without_sampling` (seed
       0x3E30_BED5_BED5_0404): shutdown flag set before entry;
       asserts Green tier handle (no sample taken), pinning the shared
       shutdown/Cx termination contract across all three sampling
       monitors.

   Observation choice: memory_pressure.rs has no sample counter on its
   telemetry struct (unlike cpu_pressure / memory_budget), so the tests
   observe the atomic `latest_tier` handle. The initial value is Green
   and a real sample would classify based on actual system memory, so
   "tier still Green" is a strong signal that no sample was taken
   (necessary-but-not-sufficient; paired with the shutdown test it
   pins the short-circuit contract).

Verification (CC=llvm clang, CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-rusticmaple-target):

  * cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib memory_pressure::: 41/41 pass
    (39 existing + 2 new LabRuntime)
  * cargo clippy -p frankenterm-core --lib -D warnings: clean
  * rustfmt --check: clean

Session tally now covers all three sampling monitors:
  tick 3: cpu_pressure     (2ee8a04b)
  tick 4: memory_budget    (3e7c6f0f)
  tick 5: memory_pressure  (this commit)

Leaves ft-xbnl0.2.3 still in_progress — remaining Cx-first candidates
are non-sampling-loop surfaces.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
